Topic: List

append - Adds one item to the end of the list.
extend - Adds all items from another iterable to the end of the list.
insert - Inserts an item at a specific index.
remove - Removes the first matching item by value.
pop - Removes and returns an item by index (last item by default).
clear - Removes all items from the list.
index - Returns the index of the first matching item.
count - Returns how many times a value appears in the list.
sort - Sorts the list in place (ascending by default).
reverse - Reverses the order of items in place.
copy - Returns a shallow copy of the list.

Topic: Tuple

tuple (overview) - Ordered, immutable collection that allows duplicate values.
count - Returns how many times a value appears in the tuple.
index - Returns the index of the first matching value.
packing - Combines multiple values into a single tuple.
unpacking - Assigns tuple values to multiple variables.
extended unpacking (*) - Captures multiple middle values into a list.
tuple() - Converts an iterable (like list) into a tuple.

Topic: Set

set (overview) - Unordered collection of unique values. No duplicates allowed.
add - Adds one item to the set.
update - Adds multiple items from an iterable to the set.
remove - Removes an item by value. Raises error if not found.
discard - Removes an item safely. No error if value does not exist.
pop - Removes and returns an arbitrary item (order is unpredictable).
clear - Removes all items from the set.
copy - Returns a shallow copy of the set.
union (|) - Returns all unique items from both sets.
intersection (&) - Returns only items common to both sets.
difference (-) - Returns items in first set but not in second.
symmetric difference (^) - Returns items in either set but not both.
issubset - Checks if all items of one set exist in another.
issuperset - Checks if a set contains all items of another.
isdisjoint - Returns True if two sets share no common items.
set() - Converts an iterable to a set (removes duplicates).

Topic: Frozenset

frozenset (overview) - Immutable, unordered collection of unique values.
frozenset() - Creates an immutable set from an iterable.
in - Fast membership check in frozenset.
| (union) - Returns all unique items from both frozensets.
& (intersection) - Returns common items from both frozensets.
- (difference) - Returns items in first frozenset but not second.
^ (symmetric difference) - Returns items in either frozenset but not both.
issubset - Checks if all values of one frozenset exist in another.
issuperset - Checks if one frozenset contains all values of another.
isdisjoint - Returns True when two collections have no common values.
use case - Can be used as dictionary keys and inside sets because it is hashable.

Topic: Hashable & Unhashable

hashable (overview) - Value with stable hash; can be used in set and as dict key.
unhashable (overview) - Value without stable hash; cannot be used in set or as dict key.
hashable examples - int, str, bool, tuple (with hashable items), frozenset.
unhashable examples - list, dict, set.
key rule - Mutable types are usually unhashable; immutable types are usually hashable.
tuple rule - Tuple is hashable only if all inner elements are hashable.
nested set rule - Use frozenset inside set because regular set is unhashable.
hash() - Built-in function used to check hashability (raises TypeError for unhashable values).

Topic: Dictionary

dict (overview) - Mutable key-value mapping collection. Keys must be unique and hashable.
dict[key] - Direct access to value by key (raises KeyError if missing).
get(key, default=None) - Safe access to value with optional default if key missing.
in - Checks if key exists in dictionary.
update(dict) - Adds or updates multiple key-value pairs in one operation.
setdefault(key, default) - Returns value if key exists, else adds key with default.
pop(key, default=None) - Removes key and returns its value (safe with default).
popitem() - Removes and returns the last inserted key-value pair.
del - Directly removes a key from the dictionary.
clear() - Removes all items from dictionary.
keys() - Returns an iterator over all dictionary keys.
values() - Returns an iterator over all dictionary values.
items() - Returns an iterator over all key-value pairs (most useful for looping).
copy() - Returns a shallow copy of the dictionary.
fromkeys(iterable, value) - Creates a new dict with keys from iterable, all values set to value.
len() - Returns the number of key-value pairs in dictionary.
merge (|) - Merges two dicts with union operator (Python 3.9+).
dict comprehension - Create dict from iterable using {k: v for k, v in iterable}.
nested dict - Dictionary containing other dictionaries as values.
use case - Ideal for API responses, configs, test metadata, key-value lookups.
